cross_scene_arcs.title: Material Security vs. Emotional Safety
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Flan repeatedly prioritizes material and financial stability over emotional or physical safety, from checking valuables after a break-in to admitting he nearly lost a key artwork due to financial strain.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: emotionally exposed but still focused on asset preservation
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: tendency to withhold financial and emotional stress from Ouisa until a crisis point

cross_scene_arcs.title: Flan's Paternal Denial and Identity Conflict
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Flan remains unaware or in denial of his biological relationship with Paul, treating him as a stranger while continuing to make decisions that reject or avoid him, even as the truth surfaces.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Paul's attempts to contact Flan are rebuffed, and he remains unacknowledged by his father

cross_scene_arcs.title: Art as Commerce vs. Art as Meaning
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Flan grapples with the dual role of art in his life—both as a financial instrument under pressure and as a source of personal, emotional resonance revealed in a dream of iconic paintings.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: tension between art as commerce and art as meaning

cross_scene_arcs.title: Escalating Marital Fracture
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Flan and Ouisa's relationship deteriorates from tension over risk and emotional suppression to open conflict and mutual recognition of incompatibility.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: tension with Ouisa over risk perception
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: emotional distance and divergent values regarding money and intimacy

cross_scene_arcs.title: Moral Disengagement in Crisis
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Flan shifts from direct caretaking of Paul to active resistance and emotional withdrawal, ultimately opposing Ouisa’s moral commitment and disengaging after Paul's arrest.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: personally involved in Paul's recovery and comfort

cross_scene_arcs.title: Quest for Truth and Authenticity
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Ouisa moves from insisting on truth in storytelling to actively investigating deception, confronting institutional and familial resistance, and ultimately rejecting false narratives in personal and marital life.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: insists on naming Sidney Poitier despite libel concerns
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: declares her marriage to Flan fundamentally false and incompatible
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: how to live authentically in a world built on performance and privilege

cross_scene_arcs.title: Emotional Bond with Paul
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Ouisa's relationship with Paul evolves from symbolic idealization in a dream to active emotional pursuit, as she defends, protects, and seeks him despite his criminal actions and lack of formal identity.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: dreams of Paul as Sidney Poitier, imbuing him with artistic and philosophical meaning
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: fails to locate Paul in the system, realizing their bond has no institutional recognition
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: her emotional investment in Paul cannot be validated or acted upon without legal or familial standing

cross_scene_arcs.title: Shattered Safety and Reasserted Control
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Ouisa responds to repeated violations of her domestic and emotional safety by asserting control through aesthetic order, intellectual performance, and ultimately, moral and emotional independence.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: restores composure by rehanging a Kandinsky after a break-in
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: declares her marriage a 'terrible match,' rejecting emotional dependence on Flan
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: ongoing fear of violence and vulnerability beneath attempts to assert control

cross_scene_arcs.title: Conflict Over Investigation and Authority
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Ouisa's determination to uncover the truth about Paul brings her into conflict with Flan, Larkin, and her children, escalating from disagreement to open defiance and emotional rupture.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: aligns with Flan and Kitty to investigate Paul's identity
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: defies Flan and children, asserting her own moral authority despite isolation
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: whether her pursuit of Paul stems from justice, control, or emotional need

cross_scene_arcs.title: Performance and Resistance Under Observation
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Paul performs for an unseen observer in a controlled white room, beginning with compliance and evolving into physical defiance and philosophical assertion, shifting from subject to authoritative voice.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: positioned as a subject performing under observation
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Paul is positioned as a moral and philosophical guide
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Paul's intensity and performative authority begin to feel slightly unsettling or excessive

cross_scene_arcs.title: Infiltration and Expulsion from the Kittredge Household
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Paul gains entry to the Kittredge home through a fabricated mugging story, builds trust by expressing emotional connection to their children and cultural insight, then is expelled after admitting he endangered them out of loneliness and fear.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: accepted into the Kittredges' home while injured and dispossessed
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Paul is no longer welcome in the Kittredge home
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Paul's fear of his father and emotional isolation remain unaddressed

cross_scene_arcs.title: Revelation of Identity and Familial Rejection
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Paul reveals he is Flan Kittredge’s estranged son, born from a past relationship tied to civil rights activism, and continues to live in material hardship and isolation despite his lineage, rejecting offers of shelter.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: publicly acknowledges identity as son of a wealthy art dealer
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: remains unhoused, carrying belongings in plastic bags
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: inability to accept help or form lasting bonds

cross_scene_arcs.title: Confrontation with Racial and National Identity
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Paul articulates his alienation from American identity despite experiencing racism, later disclosing his full hyphenated name and racial identity during a vulnerable call for help, culminating in arrest and a final gesture of betrayal.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Paul expresses personal conflict about racial identity and national belonging
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: in police custody and separated from the Kittredges, with no immediate support or protection
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: the meaning and significance of the Kandinsky painting remains unclear

cross_scene_arcs.title: Quest for Recognition and Failed Reconciliation
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Paul seeks recognition and protection from both his biological father and the Kittredge family, but is repeatedly rejected; he ultimately turns to Ouisa for personal intervention, only to be arrested and betrayed.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: to avoid his father finding out and to seek forgiveness
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: in police custody and separated from the Kittredges, with no immediate support or protection
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether Ouisa will follow through by personally taking Paul to the police as he requests
